channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Xbox Games Showcase Announced for June 2026
item.content:encoded:
A new announcement reveals that an Xbox Games Showcase is arriving on June 7, 2026, followed by a Gears of War E-Day Direct.

We just had an Xbox Partner Preview event earlier this month, where players received announcements for several big games, including Stalker 2, Dispatch, Hades 2, and more. Now, they seem to be gearing up for their biggest showcase of the year, which arrives in just over 2 months.

Xbox Games Showcase 2026 and Gears of War E-Day Direct Set for June 2026

A new post on the Xbox Wire reveals that the Xbox Games Showcase 2026 will premiere on June 7 at 10 am Pacific/1 pm Eastern 6 pm UK time. The event will be followed directly by a Gears of War E-Day Direct, providing players with a deep dive into the upcoming title’s new details, gameplay, and insights into the origin story. The event will include first looks at gameplay, news from first-party studios and third-party partners, and more.

Moreover, as it is the 25th anniversary of Xbox, they have decided to bring back Xbox FanFest at the showcase, where players will look back at the colourful 25 years and get a glimpse into the future. If you can’t attend the event in person, the showcase will be livestreamed in “over 40 languages, as well as American Sign Language, British Sign Language, and English Audio Descriptions.” Interested readers can tune into YouTube, Twitch, or Facebook to catch the livestream.

In other news, there are rumors of a new Xbox Game Pass tier from Microsoft that would offer only first-party games. Additionally, the reason that new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma retired the “This Is An Xbox” campaign has been revealed. Are you excited for the Xbox Games Showcase 2026 and Gears of War E-Day Direct arriving in June? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about a Zelda-themed Switch 2 console reportedly being worked on. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Unseen Grand Theft Auto 4 Dev Kit Assets Have Leaked
item.content:encoded:
A GTA 4 dev kit from early in the game’s development has been leaked via the GTA Forums, and it reveals a bunch of models and assets that never made it into the final release.

You just know that die-hard fans will find a way to learn about their favorite media no matter what. This is why it’s no surprise to see a full GTA 4 kit discovered. It reveals new vehicles, characters, and more that were either tweaked for launch or were omitted altogether.

GTA 4 Dev Kit Leaks

GTA Forums user Pingwinek shared a full Grand Theft Auto 4 dev kit, featuring over 100 files.

An 11-year-old thread on the GTA Forums continues to keep the dream alive in the search for GTA 4 info. Pingwinek shared the zip file on March 28, and the list of contents is long.

It has countless audio samples, GTA 4 beta elements, animations, models, and more. The GTA Forums post has sifted through them, and found Ferry models, new Radio Stations, and tons of cut models.

You can check out the full extent of the GTA 4 dev kit right here.

With the eternal wait for Grand Theft Auto 6 going on, this might be a fun distraction to kill some time!

Grand Theft Auto 4 might be nearly 20 years old, but it doesn’t make finding out new details about the game any less fun. What’s your favorite part about these early GTA 4 assets and plans? What’s your favorite fun fact about GTA 4? Share your thoughts and feelings via the Insider Gaming Discord.

In case you missed it earlier this year, check out the new GTA 6 ‘leak’ containing alleged footage forgotten for years.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Zelda-Themed Switch 2 Console On The Way, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
A new claim states that a limited-edition Zelda-themed Switch 2 is in the works.

There’s a chance you might not have heard that some of the biggest titles from Nintendo’s 2026 lineup leaked recently, including a major Zelda remake. The remake is seemingly going to be of one of the highest-rated games of all time, The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time, and Nintendo might have something special planned for the Switch 2 release.

Switch 2 Could Seemingly Get a Zelda Remake-Themed Edition, and the Remake Reportedly has a “Pretty Big Budget”

Speshal Nick recently shared a claim on X (formerly Twitter) regarding a Zelda-themed Limited Edition Switch 2 console. In his latest post, he mentioned being told that “we’re getting a Zelda-themed Limited Edition Switch 2 console.” However, he noted that he is unsure if the game will be offered as a bundle with the console. Additionally, Nick included a screenshot in his post indicating that he was informed that The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time Remake has a “pretty big budget.”

A themed Switch 2 console is on many players’ wishlists, and what better game to receive a limited edition than Ocarina of Time? We’ve seen several themed editions for the original Switch, including models like the Standard, Lite, and OLED. Among these limited editions, The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom version is arguably the best-looking, with other notable contenders including Mario Red, Pokémon Scarlet & Violet, Super Smash Bros. Ultimate, and more editions.

In other news, the voice behind Princess Zelda has discussed the direction and expectations, or lack thereof, she was given during her audition. Additionally, Netflix has secured exclusive streaming rights to Sony’s live-action Legend of Zelda movie following its theatrical debut in May 2027. What are your thoughts on a limited edition Zelda-themed Switch 2 console? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Marathon developers committing to support the game for “many years”. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: When is Fortnite OG Season 8? Release Date & Expected Start Time
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite continues to roll back the years with regular updates to Fortnite OG, and Season 8 of the popular game mode is right around the corner.

Fortnite OG began as a limited-time event celebrating the first-ever season of Fortnite, before returning as a separate game mode entirely, deploying flashback versions of popular seasons in the past.

Season 8 is next on the agenda for Fortnite OG, and Insider Gaming has all the important launch details you need.

When is Fortnite OG Season 8?

Fortnite Season 8 launches on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, following Epic Games’ deployment of version 40.10 of the game.

As usual for major updates like this, April 1 will begin with server downtime while the new version is deployed, followed by a return to service with a slew of new content across various modes.

  • More Insider Gaming: When is The Next Fortnite Update?

While there will be plenty added to Fortnite across all modes, the standout additions will come in Fortnite OG, with a new Battle Pass, map overhauls, new weapons, and, of course, the return of the beloved Avengers LTM.

The launch of Fortnite OG Season 8 kicks off the next rewind season for the Epic Games title, and is expected to last for around two months.

What Time Does Fortnite OG Season 8 Release?

Fortnite downtime is on a fairly rigid structure, with servers set to go offline for the launch of OG Season 8 at 1 AM PT/4 AM ET on April 1.

Naturally, that means a later time for those elsewhere in the world, including in Europe and Australia, and the exact time that the game will be available again is not set in stone.

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Teases Return of One of The All-Time Greatest Collabs

Due to the maintenance period, Epic Games doesn’t share specific times for the servers to return, and instead leaves it flexible in case of any delays. However, mid-season patches like this one usually result in downtime of around two to three hours.

As such, servers should be accessible for Fortnite OG Season 7 around 4 AM PT/7 AM ET, allowing for a three-hour window for the patch to be deployed, but the best bet is to keep an eye on social media for full details.

What are you most excited for in Fortnite OG Season 8? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, take a look at all Week 2 Quests in Chapter 7, Season 2, and check out when Rocket Racing is being removed entirely.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-og-season-8-release-date-start-times/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: When is The Next Fortnite Update? Fortnite V40.10, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite’s regular content drops are continuing, and while it’s only been a few weeks since Chapter 7, Season 2 kicked off, a fresh update is on the horizon.

The Battle Royale behemoth is enduring a bit of a turbulent period following Epic Games‘ mass layoffs, which have led to players raising concerns about the long-term stability of the title, but Fortnite is not slowing down anytime soon.

Fortnite v40.10 is due to arrive this week, and Insider Gaming has all the important details on exactly when it drops and what you can expect to be added.

When is The Next Fortnite Update?

Fortnite’s next update is set to arrive on Wednesday, April 1, when Epic Games deploys v40.10 into the fold.

Epic Games is pretty settled into regular updates, deploying major updates alongside downtime every two weeks. With the launch of the latest season on March 19, it’s now time for a new update and a fresh slew of additional content in the game.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 2 Quests

It’s far from the end of Chapter 7, Season 2, however, which is due to continue until early June, but it will mark the start of a new season in Fortnite OG.

What’s Coming in Fortnite v40.10?

The biggest drop in Fortnite’s v40.10 update is undoubtedly the start of a new season in Fortnite OG, which is making the transition into Season 8.

Fortnite OG Season 1 rolls the clock back to 2019, providing revamped content based on Fortnite Chapter 1, Season 8, including the return of the popular Avengers: Endgame LTM mode that pitted the Avengers and the Chitauri against each other.

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Teases Return of One of The All-Time Greatest Collabs

Additionally, skins are coming to the shop for the Italian Brainrot collab, including Tung Tung Sahur and Ballerina Cappuccina, alongside a Super Meat Boy 3D sidekick.

The Chapter 7 Season 2 Battle Pass will also see bonus reward Super Styles unlocked, alongside a slew of leaks concerning Save The World’s transition into free-to-play.

Additional leaks are also likely to surface, showcasing what to expect in the next Fortnite Festival season and the upcoming LEGO Fortnite Battle Pass, alongside the reveal of further upcoming collabs.

Are you excited for Season 8 in Fortnite OG? Share your thoughts on the latest update in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, find out when Festival Battle Stage is being removed, and check out all the details from the Epic Games layoffs.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/when-is-the-next-fortnite-update-v40-10/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Black Ops 7 Director Addresses Skill-Based Damage Accusations
item.content:encoded:
Skill-based matchmaking conversations have dominated the majority of Black Ops 7, but a recent trend has seen the community talking about a different type of gameplay influence, namely skill-based damage differences.

Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 launched with open matchmaking, AKA no SBMM. Meaning, your lobbies are not determined by stats and player ability (for the most part). However, SBMM has factored into the game more as time has gone on, and players are now accusing Black Ops 7 of having skill-based damage. A Treyarch dev has shut down these suggestions.

Skill-Based Damage Not Used in Black Ops 7

Treyarch Design Director Matt Scronce has responded to Reidboyy’s post that ‘skill-based damage is 100% real and in the game.’

Scronce had this to say about the topic:

“I can promise you, there’s nothing behind the scenes modifying any of our damage values.”

So, if you feel you’re getting different damage output compared to other people in your lobby, this is merely a figment of your imagination. Your lobby might determine who you’re put against, but the only thing affecting your damage is potentially ping or netcode.

It came about because of a Pirat_Nation post mentioning Sony’s patent that dynamically affects gameplay for different players on the fly. In theory, this WOULD be skill-based damage, and could help players floundering in a match, or give someone dominating a lobby a roadblock.

You can check out the full exchange right here.

SBMM is one thing, but how does skill-based damage sound to you? If it’s implemented across different multiplayer games, including CoD, would it stop you from partaking in online shooters or multiplayer in general? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out the full Black Ops 7 & Warzone Season 3 Release Date & Start Times for more CoD content.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Second PS Plus Monthly Game for April Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Another addition to PlayStation Plus Monthly Games for April 2026 has been revealed, and this one will be enjoyed by anime fans: Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream.

A report from last week revealed that the PlayStation Plus Monthly Games headliner for April 2026 will be Lords of the Fallen. Now, the same insider has also provided us with the second addition for this month, along with other details, including when the games will be available to download for PS Plus Essential members in April and more.

PlayStation Plus April 2026 Monthly Games Will Introduce Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream

The latest report arrives from the reliable billbil-kun through Dealabs. It confirms that the second title for PlayStation Plus free monthly games in April is Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream. It wasn’t confirmed whether a third game will be revealed as well. The report also states that the official announcement from PlayStation should arrive on April 1, and the game should be available to download from April 7.

Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream is a new adventure where 21 characters from the SAO series join Kirito in a new battle. The title has a single-player story mod featuring an original story in which “Characters from across time and space, each with their own destiny, gather to confront the anomaly in the VR world.” There are also online modes, such as boss raids, co-op quests, and free roam.

In boss raids, players will see 20 players in five parties of four players each, take down gigantic bosses. Co-op quest has five parties of four starting together to gradually meet in a final showdown against a boss as the quest progresses. Free roam allows players to freely explore a vast map filled with powerful bosses and treasure chests.

In other news, the prices of PlayStation 5, PS5 Pro, and PlayStation Portal devices are about to increase significantly worldwide. Additionally, since the PS5 launched in 2020, PlayStation has shuttered eight of its studios, and they’re all listed here. What are your thoughts on the second addition to PlayStation Plus monthly games for April? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite teasing the return of one of the all-time greatest collabs. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
